prerequisites: Administrative access to the PrestaShop back office, which is required to enable the webservice and create an access key.
The PrestaShop Webservice enabled under Advanced Parameters > Web service (set "Enable PrestaShop Webservice" to Yes).
A webservice access key generated in the back office with at least GET permission on the resources you intend to sync (provide as the access_key config field).
The base URL of your PrestaShop store (provide as the url config field); the webservice is served from the /api/ path relative to this URL.
A start date (start_date config field) marking the earliest point from which to replicate data.

authMethods.name: Webservice access key (HTTP Basic auth)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with the access_key field, a 32-character PrestaShop Webservice key. The key is sent via HTTP Basic Authentication as the username with an empty password, so the key itself is the only credential. Generate it under Advanced Parameters > Webservice and grant it only the permissions (GET, and others as needed) required for the resources being synced.

setupSteps.title: Enable the PrestaShop Webservice
setupSteps.body: In the PrestaShop back office, go to Advanced Parameters > Web service and set "Enable PrestaShop Webservice" to Yes. The webservice must be active before any API requests will succeed.

setupSteps.title: Create a webservice access key
setupSteps.body: Under Advanced Parameters > Webservice, click "Add new webservice key" and use "Generate" to create a unique 32-character key. Set its status to enabled and grant only the permissions needed, at minimum GET on the resources you plan to read. In multistore setups, restrict the key to the appropriate shop. Copy this key into the access_key field.

setupSteps.title: Provide the store URL
setupSteps.body: Set the url field to the base URL of your PrestaShop installation (for example https://example.com or https://example.com/prestashop). The connector reaches the webservice at the /api/ entry point relative to this URL. Use HTTPS so the access key is not exposed in transit.

setupSteps.title: Set the replication start date
setupSteps.body: Set the start_date field to the earliest date from which records should be replicated. Data before this date is not pulled on the initial sync.

sources.title: PrestaShop DevDocs - Creating access to the Webservice
